task: Test the notification system. When person a in the house hold adds something assigned to person b then person b should get a notification. Also when person b completes, person a should get completed alert. Similarly test other possible alerts.

data.plan: Create comprehensive notification system tests covering the key scenarios: (1) Person A assigns a chore/task to Person B → Person B receives an assignment notification, (2) Person B completes a task assigned by Person A → Person A receives a completion notification, (3) Additional scenarios like reminder notifications, grocery item assignments, and event notifications. New test files will be added under src/__tests__/notifications/ covering assignment and completion flows using mocked Supabase and web-push. Mock handlers for household members and notification dispatch will be extended as needed. No existing source files need modification — this is purely additive test coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: When Person A creates a chore/task assigned to Person B, a push notification is sent to Person B's subscription endpoint
When Person B marks a task (assigned by Person A) as complete, Person A receives a completion push notification
When a reminder is due for Person B, Person B receives a reminder push notification
When Person A adds a grocery item assigned to Person B, Person B receives an assignment notification
Notifications are NOT sent to the person who performed the action (no self-notification)
If Person B has no push subscription, the system handles it gracefully without throwing
The notification payload contains the correct title, body, and metadata (task name, assignee, household context)
The push-subscribe endpoint correctly stores a new subscription linked to a user and household
The push-send endpoint correctly fans out notifications to all relevant household member subscriptions
The notification bell endpoint returns unread notification count and list for the authenticated user
All tests use mocked Supabase and web-push to avoid real network calls
Tests cover both success and failure/edge-case paths (e.g., expired push subscription, missing user)
